commit.message:
Fix empty benchmark results: NIST CTC manifest YAML block scalar breaks custom parser (#42)

* Initial plan

* Fix empty benchmark results: convert NIST CTC manifest summary from YAML block scalar to single-line string

The custom YAML subset parser in fixture_manifest.cc does not support
YAML block scalars (> or |). The nist-ctc/manifest.yaml used:

  summary: >
    NIST MBE PMI Conformance Test Case ...

The parser reads the second indented line and throws "Unexpected
indentation", which bench_navigator catches as a hard manifest parse
error. Because HasErrors() returns true, bench_navigator exits with
EXIT_FAILURE before printing the "=== Fixture Ray Benchmark Results ==="
header. The generate_bench_report.py parser finds no header and renders
an empty page.

Fix: convert the summary to a single-line quoted string, consistent with
all other family manifests (boolean-compound, direct-primitives,
profile-faceted, tessellated, twisted-swept, wrapper-decorator).

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(security): resolve 37 OSV vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies

Root cause: @cyclonedx/cdxgen 11.11.0 pulled in vulnerable transitive
dependencies (tar, undici, minimatch, sequelize, lodash, etc.)

Fixes:
- Upgrade @cyclonedx/cdxgen 11.11.0 → 12.1.2 (resolves 28 vulns)
- Add pnpm overrides for remaining transitive vulnerabilities:
  - tar >=7.5.11 (6 CVEs including path traversal)
  - minimatch >=3.1.3/7.4.8/9.0.7 (ReDoS)
  - flatted >=3.4.0 (from eslint's flat-cache)
  - rollup >=4.59.0 (from vite)
  - lodash >=4.17.23 (prototype pollution)
- pnpm audit now reports zero vulnerabilities

Note: ajv 6.12.6 (eslint transitive) cannot be overridden — 6.14.0
breaks eslint's internal API. This is a known eslint issue fixed in
eslint 10.x.

commit.message:
Add three.js point-cloud visualization of geantino ray tests to CI artifacts (#40)

* Initial plan

* Add three.js point-cloud visualization of geantino ray tests

- Extend FixtureRayComparisonOptions with point_cloud_dir: when set,
  CompareFixtureRays writes a per-fixture JSON file containing
  pre_step_origin and native/imported post-step surface hit points
- Add WritePointCloudJson / JsonString / WriteJsonVectorArray helpers
  in fixture_ray_compare.cc (anonymous namespace, <fstream> added)
- bench_navigator accepts an optional third positional arg for the
  point-cloud output directory
- New .github/scripts/generate_point_cloud_report.py generates a
  self-contained HTML viewer backed by three.js (v0.169, CDN) with
  OrbitControls, fixture dropdown, native/imported toggle, and stats
- ci.yml: run bench_navigator with point-cloud export, generate bench
  and point-cloud HTML reports, upload as ci-reports artifact

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Refactor point-cloud report: separate CSS/JS files + Jinja2 template

Extract embedded CSS and JS from generate_point_cloud_report.py into
proper tracked files:
- point_cloud_viewer.css  – styles with CSS SPDX header
- point_cloud_viewer.js   – three.js viewer; reads fixture data from
  a <script type="application/json" id="fixture-data"> DOM element,
  removing the /*FIXTURE_DATA*/ string replacement entirely
- point_cloud_viewer.html.jinja2 – Jinja2 HTML template that inlines
  the CSS/JS and embeds the per-run fixture JSON

generate_point_cloud_report.py now uses Jinja2 (Environment +
FileSystemLoader) to render the template.  ci.yml installs jinja2
before the report-generation step.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

* Apply review feedback on point-cloud export and viewer

- fixture_ray_compare.cc: JsonString() escapes control chars (\\uXXXX,
  uppercase hex per RFC 8259); include <cstdio> for snprintf
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: fixture_id in JSON is now family-qualified
  (family/id) so viewer dropdown grouping works correctly
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: filename sanitization replaces all
  non-(alnum/hyphen/period) chars with '_'; drops undocumented
  "only alphanumerics" comment
- fixture_ray_compare.cc: WritePointCloudJson exports separate
  native_pre_step_origin and imported_pre_step_origin fields to
  eliminate metadata inconsistency
- point_cloud_viewer.js: clearClouds() now disposes both geometry and
  material to prevent GPU resource leaks across fixture loads
- point_cloud_viewer.js: camera fitting prefers currently-visible cloud
  (respects showNative/showImported flags)
- point_cloud_viewer.js: stats panel shows both native and imported
  origins
- point_cloud_viewer.html.jinja2: SPDX header changed from Jinja2
  {# #} comments to HTML <!-- --> form for enarx/spdx CI compliance
- generate_point_cloud_report.py: fixture_json escapes '</' -> '<\/'
  before template injection to prevent </script> injection; docstring
  drops "self-contained" claim and documents CDN dependency; key
  validation updated for new origins schema

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add __pycache__ to .gitignore and remove accidentally committed .pyc file

commit.message:
fix: convert MongoDB unit tests to use NSubstitute mocks (#52)

* fix: convert MongoDB unit tests to use NSubstitute mocks

This PR fixes failing MongoDB unit tests by converting them from real
database connections to proper NSubstitute mocks.

## Changes

### New Infrastructure
- Add IIssueTrackerDbContext interface for mockable DbContext
- Add MockDbSetHelper for creating mock DbSet instances
- Add RepositoryTestBase<T> abstract base class for common test setup
- Add example tests demonstrating mock usage patterns

### Refactored Tests
- RepositoryGetByIdTests (6 tests)
- RepositoryGetAllTests (2 tests)
- RepositoryFindTests (2 tests)
- RepositoryFirstOrDefaultTests (2 tests)
- RepositoryAddTests (4 tests)
- RepositoryAddRangeTests (4 tests)
- RepositoryUpdateTests (4 tests)
- RepositoryDeleteTests (4 tests)
- RepositoryAnyTests (2 tests)
- RepositoryCountTests (3 tests)
- RepositoryConstructorTests (6 tests)

### Removed
- MongoDbTestFixture.cs (no longer needed for unit tests)

## Results
- All 77 MongoDB unit tests now pass without MongoDB
- Tests run in ~300ms (vs connection timeout failures)
- CI will no longer require MongoDB for unit test job

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: register IIssueTrackerDbContext in DI container

The Repository<T> class now depends on IIssueTrackerDbContext interface.
Added service registration to map the interface to the concrete DbContext.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve integration test flakiness and CI compatibility

- MongoDB: Give each concurrent task its own DbContext to avoid
  SaveChangesAsync race conditions in ConcurrentAdds_Should_NotConflict
- AzureStorage: Allow AzuriteFixture to use AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING
  from CI environment instead of always starting a Testcontainer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: initialize db once before concurrent add operations

The concurrent test was initializing the database inside each task,
causing race conditions. Initialize once before spawning concurrent tasks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: share db name in concurrent test, enable Azurite loose mode

- MongoDB concurrent test: All contexts now share the same database name
  so entities are actually persisted to the same database
- Azurite CI: Enable --loose mode to relax authentication checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use Testcontainers for Azure integration tests in CI

Reverted to pure Testcontainers approach for Azure Storage tests.
Manual Azurite setup in CI was causing 403 auth errors.
Testcontainers handles container lifecycle reliably.

Also fixed MongoDB concurrent test to use shared database name.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Azure integration tests to use authenticated blob clients

- Use BlobServiceClient from fixture instead of unauthenticated BlobClient(Uri)
- Extract blob name from URL using Skip(2) for Azurite format (account+container)
- All 25 Azure Storage integration tests now pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The platform-engineers SSO permission set was missing identitystore
permissions needed by the organization module to resolve admin user
emails via aws_identitystore_user data sources, causing permission
denied errors when running terragrunt plan.

Add identitystore:GetUserId and identitystore:DescribeUser to the
cfn-tf-deploy policy. These are the minimum permissions required.

commit.message:
dparser: merge consecutive RParen pairs into DoubleRParen when closing ArithSubst (#224)

Two separate `)` tokens closing an `ArithSubst` (`$((`) had no single
unambiguous closing token, breaking syntax highlighting matching between
`$((` and `))`.

## Changes

- **`walk` function**: Uses a `while idx < self.tokens.len()` loop that
modifies `self.tokens` in place. Cursor-containment flags
(`token_strictly_contains_cursor`, `token_inclusively_contains_cursor`)
are computed first from the current token's original single-character
byte range. Then, when the current nesting is `ArithSubst` and the next
token is also `RParen`, `self.tokens.remove(idx + 1)` deletes the
phantom second `)` from the vector and `self.tokens[idx]` is mutated in
place (kind → `DoubleRParen`, value → `"))"`). The token is cloned into
a local variable for matching to avoid borrow conflicts when writing
back to `self.tokens[idx].annotation`. Break conditions use `break`
directly — no deferred `should_break` flag needed.

- **`nested_closing_satisfied`**: Removed `next_token` lookahead
parameter. Replaced `(RParen, ArithSubst) if next_is_rparen` with the
direct `(DoubleRParen, ArithSubst) => true` match arm.

- **Closing branch of `walk`**: `DoubleRParen` has its own arm in the
closing branch that always restores the outer command range and
continues parsing (never stops due to
`stop_parsing_at_command_boundary`). This reflects the fact that
`$((expr))` is an arithmetic value within an outer command — the outer
command is always the relevant context, regardless of where the cursor
sits inside the expression.

## Result

`$((` now gets `IsOpening(Some(closing_idx))` pointing to a single
`DoubleRParen` token (value `"))"`, `byte_range` spanning both
characters) annotated `IsClosing(opening_idx)`. The phantom second `)`
is removed from `self.tokens` in place — subsequent tokens have correct
indices as if the second `)` never existed as a separate token.

A cursor anywhere inside `$((expr))` — including on the `)` bytes
themselves — now yields the full outer command as context (including
tokens after `))`):

```
echo $((5 + 3█)) result  →  context = "echo $((5 + 3)) result"
echo $((5 + 3)█) result  →  context = "echo $((5 + 3)) result"
```

```
echo $(( bar ))
       ↑       ↑
  IsOpening  IsClosing (DoubleRParen, value = "))")
  (Some(6))    (2)
```

tokens. This causes problems with syntax highlighting.

> In the dparser, at the top of the loop if the current opening token is
the double left paren one and the current token and the next one are
both rparens, then modify the current token to be a doublerparen kind of
token, modify the value, and skip the next token also. this should
simplify the logic to determin e if the double left opening token has a
closing token.


</details>

commit.message:
Clean up glob path expansion (#168)

The old `expand_path_pattern` used a fragile sequential prefix-swap list
to reverse-transform glob results back to user-facing paths, and had no
environment variable expansion support.

### Changes

- **Split at last `/`** — separates the pattern into `raw_prefix`
(preserved verbatim for output) and `rhs_pattern` (the glob portion)
- **`expanded_prefix` pipeline** — builds the absolute path used for
globbing by delegating to bash's own filename expansion logic
- **`bash_funcs::expand_filename`** — new function that calls
`bash_expand_filename` from `bashline.c` directly via a symbol declared
in `bash_symbols.rs`; returns the original filename unchanged if
expansion fails
- **New `bash_symbols` declaration** — `bash_expand_filename`
- **Result mapping** — strips `expanded_prefix` from each glob result to
get the relative portion, then prepends `raw_prefix` to preserve the
user's original spelling in completions
- **`PathPatternExpansion` struct** — replaces the `(String,
Vec<(String, String)>)` return type with named fields

### Example

```
pattern: "$HOME/projects/fly*"
  raw_prefix:      "$HOME/projects"
  expanded_prefix: "/home/user/projects"   (after bash filename expansion)
  rhs_pattern:     "fly*"
  glob runs:       "/home/user/projects/fly*"
  result path:     "/home/user/projects/flyline"
  suggestion:      "$HOME/projects/flyline"   ← raw_prefix preserved
```

commit.message:
Skip alternate_span in formatted buffer when animations are disabled (#234)

The snake animation (`python` command easter egg) was applying
`set_alternative_span` to formatted buffer parts even when
`--disable-animations` was set, bypassing the intent of that flag.

## Change

- Added `!self.settings.disable_animations` guard to the snake animation
condition in `create_content`, so the `else` branch clears any
alternative span instead:

```rust
// Before
if self.mode.is_running() && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python") {

// After
if self.mode.is_running() && !self.settings.disable_animations && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python") {
```

commit.message:
XenoAtom.Terminal.UI session browser and pager (#18)

* Add XenoAtom.Terminal.UI session browser with --xenoatom flag

Implements a new session browser using XenoAtom.Terminal.UI's DataGrid
control with bindable row models, reactive state, and command system.
The new browser supports all existing features: Copilot CLI sessions,
Claude Code sessions, skill-validator eval DBs, search/filter, preview
panel, and session resume.

Accessible via --xenoatom or --xeno flag. Original Spectre.Console browser
remains the default.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix visual parent error: use single tree with IsVisible toggle

A Visual can only have one parent in XenoAtom.Terminal.UI. The
ComputedVisual approach shared gridScroll between two trees. Instead,
use a single HStack and toggle previewBorder.IsVisible.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix preview panel: use VStack of TextBlocks for multi-line text

TextBlock treats \n as whitespace with Wrap=true, rendering the
entire preview as one paragraph line. Replace with a VStack that
adds one TextBlock per line, rebuilt when selection changes.

Also fix preview not updating on toggle — was gated on stale
lastSelectedRow check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Enter/Escape keys, remove duplicate footer, fix stuck Loading status

- Move Open and Quit commands to grid.AddCommand() so they route before
  DataGrid's OnKeyDown consumes Enter/Escape keys
- Add explicit Escape/Quit command on the grid
- Remove redundant custom Footer (CommandBar already shows key hints)
- Remove unused statusText state; Loading indicator already in header
- Fix Loading stuck: scanComplete flag is checked reactively in header

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Enter/Escape: use grid.AddCommand for command-first dispatch

Decompilation of XenoAtom.Terminal.UI confirms:
- TerminalApp.DispatchKeyEvent calls TryHandleCommandShortcut BEFORE
  dispatching to Visual.OnKeyDown
- TryExecuteGestureCommand walks from FocusedElement up through parents
- DataGridControl.OnKeyDown does NOT consume Enter (ReadOnly mode) or
  Escape (falls through default case)

Commands registered on the grid via AddCommand are checked first in the
parent walk, before OnKeyDown runs. Also add 'q' as silent quit fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Enter/Escape: remove DataGrid built-in commands that swallow keys

Root cause: DataGridControl registers built-in commands for Enter
(DataGrid.CommitEdit) and Escape (DataGrid.CancelEdit) with
CanExecute = (_activeEditor != null). In ReadOnly mode there's never
an active editor, so CanExecute returns false. However, the command
system returns 'handled=true' even when CanExecute is false, silently
swallowing the key before our commands are checked.

Fix: call grid.RemoveCommand() to remove the built-in CommitEdit and
CancelEdit commands, then register our own Enter/Escape commands on
the grid. Since ReadOnly grids never need editor commit/cancel, this
is safe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make XenoAtom.Terminal.UI browser the default

Replace --xenoatom/--xeno opt-in flag with --classic opt-out flag.
XenoAtom browser is now used by default; pass --classic to fall
back to the Spectre.Console browser.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add XenoAtom LogControl-based pager

Replace Spectre.Console InteractivePager with XenoPager using LogControl
for built-in scrolling, search (Ctrl+F), and text selection. Falls back
to InteractivePager with --classic flag. Supports follow mode, filter
cycling, tools toggle, and info overlay.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pager width: use ZStack instead of HStack for content/info overlay

ZStack layers children at full size, so the LogControl fills the
terminal width. HStack was splitting space between visible/invisible
children.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* .squad: merge orchestration logs, decisions, cross-agent history

- Deckard: published XenoAtom feature gap assessment + 3-phase rollout plan
- Batty: implemented Phase 1 items (navigation, search cycling, preview rendering)
  * +225 lines, 0 warnings, all tests passing
  * Tech debt noted: reflection-based scroll bridge for LogControl v1.3.0 API gap
- Scribe: merged inbox entries to decisions.md, deleted inbox, updated agent history
- Created orchestration logs and session summary for audit trail

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* XenoAtom UI: Phase 1 parity, bug fixes, and review fixes

Phase 1 parity (XenoPager):
- Navigation keys: PgUp/Down, Home/End, vim j/k, g/G
- Search cycling: n/N for next/previous match
- Scroll position tracking to avoid binding context conflicts

Phase 1 parity (XenoSessionBrowser):
- Preview rendering uses dataParsers + ContentRenderer instead of raw JSONL
- Async preview loading with generation counter for responsiveness
- Branch column populated from session metadata (workspace.yaml, events.jsonl)

Bug fixes:
- Fix ScrollViewer.VerticalOffset read-then-write crash in binding context
- Fix empty branch column for filesystem-loaded sessions

Code review fixes:
- Add using statement for JsonDocument in metrics preview parsing
- Dispose JsonDocument immediately in follow mode (memory leak fix)
- Add generation-based early exit in BuildPreviewText for stale threads

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* .squad: Scribe merge — Batty Phase 2 (Xenopager h/l/0 + status bar)

Merges 3 decision inbox entries (branch fallback, horizontal offset tracking, follow/preview fixes) into canonical decisions.md. Adds orchestration log for Phase 2 spawn and session log. Updates Batty history with Phase 2 learnings.

- orchestration-log/2026-03-14T23:49:10Z-batty.md: Batty Phase 2 work summary (h/l/0 keys, enhanced header)
- log/2026-03-14T23:49:10Z-phase2-xenopager.md: Session log for phase 2
- decisions.md: Merged 3 inbox entries, now deduplicated
- agents/batty/history.md: Phase 2 completion note
- deleted: decisions/inbox/batty-*.md (3 files)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Phase 2: horizontal scroll, enhanced status bar, terminal cleanup

XenoPager:
- Add h/l/0 keys for horizontal scrolling (8-column steps)
- Binding-safe trackedHorizontalOffset pattern (write-only to ScrollViewer)
- Enhanced header: Line X/Y, Col N+ (when scrolled), match count, filter, follow
- Preserve horizontal offset across repopulates

Terminal cleanup (both XenoPager + XenoSessionBrowser):
- Console.ResetColor() after Terminal.Open() dispose to restore default fg/bg
- Console.CursorVisible = true to restore cursor
- Scoped using block in XenoSessionBrowser for proper dispose ordering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix browser fallback: Escape in XenoAtom no longer opens Spectre browser

The null-coalescing operator (??) treated XenoSessionBrowser returning
null (user pressed Escape) the same as xenoBrowser being null (classic
mode). Replace with explicit ternary to only call one browser per mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add --db --json export and fix terminal color reset on exit

- Add OutputSessionListJson for non-interactive DB session export
- Add DbSessionOutput record with metrics/judge/pairwise JSON fields
- Support 'replay --db sessions.db --json' for piped/scripted workflows
- Write ANSI reset (\x1b[0m) on exit from XenoPager and XenoSessionBrowser
  to fix invisible text after exiting fullscreen TUI
- Update help text and README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: thread-safe UI updates, reset stale eventsPath

- Queue background scan results via ConcurrentQueue, drain on UI thread
  in Terminal.Run callback to avoid cross-thread DataGrid mutation
- Reset eventsPath to empty string when no events.jsonl is found,

- All 73 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: replace reflection with InternalsVisibleTo, remove flaky stderr asserts

- Add InternalsVisibleTo("ReplayTests") so tests call internal methods directly
- Remove reflection-based test helpers for EnrichCopilotSessionMetadata/ReadClaudeBranch
- Remove strict stderr assertions that could flake on dotnet CLI noise
- All 73 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: assert exit code, use knownSessionIds for dedup

- Assert process.ExitCode == 0 in RunReplayWithArgs for clear failure diagnostics
- Use knownSessionIds HashSet to skip duplicate sessions when scanning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: upgrade maven-publish plugin to fix Central Portal deployment naming

Upgrade vanniktech/gradle-maven-publish-plugin from 0.30.0 to 0.34.0.

Version 0.30.0 generated deployment names using a random UUID
(e.g. ch.trancee-988cedbb-...) because it used UUID.randomUUID()
for Central Portal staging repositories. Version 0.32.0+ constructs
the deployment name from Maven coordinates instead:
${group}-${artifactId}-${version} (e.g. ch.trancee-noise-protocol-2.0.0).

Also removes the deprecated SonatypeHost.CENTRAL_PORTAL parameter
from publishToMavenCentral() — the Central Portal is now the default
publishing target since 0.33.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: cache all JCA providers via ThreadLocal on Android

Cache KeyPairGenerator, KeyFactory, KeyAgreement, MessageDigest, and Mac
instances using ThreadLocal to eliminate ~35 getInstance() provider lookups
per handshake. Also simplify fromPrivateKey() by reusing cached providers.

Results (ChaChaPoly_SHA256):
- Handshake: NN 382→345µs (-10%), IK 706→666µs (-6%), KK 729→687µs (-6%)
- Transport: AES-GCM 348→727 MB/s (+109%), BLAKE2 326→390 MB/s (+20%)

Updated BENCHMARK.md with platform comparison analysis explaining the
remaining 2-3× iOS/Android gap (CryptoKit hardware acceleration vs JCA
software implementation for X25519 DH).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize crypto hot paths across iOS and Android

- Cache DH public key (stored property instead of recomputing per access)
- Pre-allocate HKDF counter constants as static values
- Replace map closures with explicit for-loops in BLAKE2 HMAC ipad/opad
- Pre-size handshake output buffer (Data(capacity:256) / ByteArrayOutputStream)
- Cache JCA Cipher instances via ThreadLocal (Android)
- Use System.arraycopy for HKDF concatenation (Android)

Results (ChaChaPoly_SHA256 suite):
- iOS handshake: ~22% faster (NN: 179→129µs, XX: 358→272µs)
- Android AES-GCM transport: 2-3x faster (348→716 MB/s via Cipher caching)
- Android BLAKE2 transport: ~23% faster (326→400 MB/s)

Updated BENCHMARK.md with before/after comparison tables.
Updated CHANGELOG.md with performance section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scouting mutation generates 3 mutated encounter variations (#41)

After completing an encounter, the scouting phase now generates 3 mutated
variations of the just-completed encounter as additional choices alongside
the normal encounter deck refill. Each variation applies a random difficulty
delta ([-0.15, +0.30]) to encounter parameters and mutates ~20% of enemy
deck cards while preserving total card count.

Key changes:
- New scouting.rs module with mutation logic for all 8 disciplines
- GameState captures last_encounter_kind in all discipline finish functions
- EncounterApplyScouting handler generates mutations + normal deck refill
- EncounterPickEncounter handler cleans up un-selected mutation cards
- 3 mutation operations: ScaleValues, RedistributeCopies, SwapTier
- Combat merges 3 decks for 20% selection; herbalism uses characteristic
  mutations; fishing also scales FishCard.value
- Updated tutorial, hints, and designer docs with scouting mutation info
- 3 new integration tests (136 total, 82.39% coverage)

commit.message:
Milestone reuse discipline effects (#40)

* BREAKING: Milestones reuse discipline card effects with best values

Milestone encounters no longer define their own separate card effects.
Instead, they look up the discipline's existing EnemyCardEffects and use
roll_best_concrete_effect (max values) to create the most powerful
version of each effect.

Key changes:
- Add tier: u32 field to LibraryCard for tier-aware effect lookups
- Add roll_best_concrete_effect() that always picks max values
- Add Library methods: card_effects_for_discipline_and_tier,
  enemy_effects_for_discipline_and_tier, add_card_with_tier
- Refactor all 5 register_*_milestone functions to derive from
  the discipline's base encounter definition instead of hardcoding
- Combat milestones classify enemy effects into deck categories
- Gathering milestones (mining/herbalism/woodcutting/fishing) extract
  the base encounter structure and rebuild with best-rolled effects
- generate_milestone_reward_effects now also scales EnemyCardEffects
  and tags new effects with the next tier number

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for milestone effect reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve milestone deck composition on tier escalation

When a player beats a milestone and the next tier is generated, the new
milestone now mirrors the defeated milestone's deck structure instead of
rebuilding from scratch or from the base encounter.

Key changes:
- Add Library::replace_card() for in-place card replacement at same ID
- Generalize tier_effect_mapping() to accept from_tier/to_tier
- All 5 register_*_milestone functions accept optional previous def
- Combat milestone preserves per-deck card types and hand counts
- Non-combat milestones derive from defeated milestone (not base encounter)
- handle_milestone_finish extracts defeated inner encounter before replace
- Use scale_factor() consistently for tier scaling ratios
- Add 2 integration tests: deck composition + card ID preservation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
